channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: The Tate brothers are arrested in Miami, U.S. Marshals Service tells AP
item.description: The brothers were taken into custody on a sealed warrant, the Marshals Service told the AP. British prosecutors said they were seeking the extradition of the brothers on rape and trafficking charges.
item.content:encoded:
The brothers were taken into custody on a sealed warrant, the Marshals Service told the AP. British prosecutors said they were seeking the extradition of the brothers on rape and trafficking charges.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Josh Kerr of Britain breaks 27-year-old world record in the mile
item.description: Kerr announced his bid to break the world record months in advance, and pulled off the plan in London on Saturday.
item.content:encoded:
Kerr announced his bid to break the world record months in advance, and pulled off the plan in London on Saturday.

(Image credit: Harry Murphy)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: Air quality improving in Northeast, while wildfire smoke hangs over Midwest
item.description: Wildfire smoke and unhealthy air persist and shift direction, as Trump demands explanation from Canada.
item.content:encoded:
Wildfire smoke and unhealthy air persist and shift direction, as Trump demands explanation from Canada.

(Image credit: Finn Gomez/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Kristin Wright

item.title: U.S. launches new airstrikes to 'punish' Iran for troop deaths
item.description: Two U.S. service members were killed in action while "partner forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks," U.S. Central Command said Saturday.
item.content:encoded:
Two U.S. service members were killed in action while "partner forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks," U.S. Central Command said Saturday.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Finding World Cup joy in speaking to women who love soccer
item.description: Tune into World Cup coverage, and you are likely to see waves of male, screaming, sweaty fans. But one of the joys of covering this World Cup has been speaking to women who love soccer.
item.content:encoded:
Tune into World Cup coverage, and you are likely to see waves of male, screaming, sweaty fans. But one of the joys of covering this World Cup has been speaking to women who love soccer.

(Image credit: Danielle Villasana for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?
item.description: U.S. presidents have promised short, decisive wars in Afghanistan, Iraq and Iran. All have proved much more difficult than advertised and fallen far short of the political goals set at the beginning.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. presidents have promised short, decisive wars in Afghanistan, Iraq and Iran. All have proved much more difficult than advertised and fallen far short of the political goals set at the beginning.

(Image credit: Brennan Linsley)
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Opinion: The continued courage of Captain Sully
item.description: Retired pilot Chesley "Sully" Sullenberger is known for safely landing an airplane on the Hudson River in 2009. This week, he announced that he has Alzheimer's disease.
item.content:encoded:
Retired pilot Chesley "Sully" Sullenberger is known for safely landing an airplane on the Hudson River in 2009. This week, he announced that he has Alzheimer's disease.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: 8 killed and more than 60 wounded in Ukrainian drone attack on Russian regions
item.description: Kyiv's forces are continuing their aerial campaign against energy infrastructure and military targets inside Russia, aiming to undermine Moscow's war effort.
item.content:encoded:
Kyiv's forces are continuing their aerial campaign against energy infrastructure and military targets inside Russia, aiming to undermine Moscow's war effort.

(Image credit: Moscow Region Governor Andrei Vo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The 2000s called. They want their digital camera back
item.description: Why are people who weren't born 25 years ago snapping up the digital camera of that era? Blame Taylor Swift, trend cycles, childhood nostalgia and smartphone fatigue.
item.content:encoded: Why are people who weren't born 25 years ago snapping up the digital camera of that era? Blame Taylor Swift, trend cycles, childhood nostalgia and smartphone fatigue.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: This unusual, Johnny Carson-backed fund has helped Nebraska arts survive — until now
item.description: A unique public-private endowment has long shielded Nebraska's cultural scene from federal budget cuts. But a massive state deficit is putting its future in jeopardy.
item.content:encoded:
A unique public-private endowment has long shielded Nebraska's cultural scene from federal budget cuts. But a massive state deficit is putting its future in jeopardy.

(Image credit: Walker Pickering for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman
